results.possibleIdentity: James A. Crawford was an engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, primarily associated with ITT Federal Laboratories (also known as ITT Gilfillan) in the San Fernando Valley region of California. He was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. His technical expertise was exceptionally broad, covering fields such as electrical, mechanical, and structural engineering, mathematics, physics, and computer information systems. According to his professional records from the period, his specialized technical skills included work with optics, lasers, fiber optics, audio systems, tape recording, digital systems, instrumentation and control systems, circuit design, and integrated circuits. He was also interested in interdisciplinary applications such as environmental simulation, architecture, and industrial design. During his career, he was based in the San Fernando and Lake View Terrace areas of California, where ITT Federal Laboratories maintained significant facilities, including the ITT Gilfillan plant which specialized in radar and advanced electronics.
